description: Content specifically targets the pre-lunch or lunchtime window — typically 11AM-1PM Saudi time — with messaging that acknowledges the midday meal occasion and provides a timely ordering or visit CTA.
structural_recipe: Post timing: 10:30-11:30AM Saudi time. Caption: 'وقت الغدا وين؟' / 'غداك جاهز؟' / 'يلا الغدا'. Urgency: 'تحكم قبل ما تخلص'. Tone: casual, practical, hunger-activating. Visual: lunch-specific dishes or full meals. Works best as Stories content paired with quick ordering CTA.
why_it_works: Daypart targeting is among the most efficient conversion tactics in F&B social marketing. A post about lunch, posted at lunchtime, reaches people who are actively thinking about their midday meal — the intent-to-purchase moment. Saudi lunchtime is a compressed decision window (12-1PM) — being present in that window with a clear offering is high-value.
transplantation_caveats: Timing is critical — a lunch post at 2PM misses the decision window entirely.
Content must be actionable — 'where are you going for lunch?' prompts decision; 'we have great lunch options' does not.
Delivery ordering CTAs are highest-converting for this daypart.
Don't apply lunch framing to products not appropriate for lunch occasions.

provenance.source: corpus_synthesis_474_obs — referenced in obs but previously undefined
